center.name: 森莺科
center.summary: 森莺科是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括Louisiana waterthrush、食蟲鶯、Tennessee warbler等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: motacilla | synonyms = Seiurus motacilla }} thumb|Singing from a perch near an audible brook, Glastonbury, CT thumb The Louisiana waterthrush (Parkesia motacilla) is a New World warbler, that breeds in eastern North America and winters in the West Indies and Central America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: luciae | synonyms = Vermivora luciae Oreothlypis luciae }} Lucy's warbler (Leiothlypis luciae) is a small New World warbler found in North America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Nashville warbler (Leiothlypis ruficapilla) is a small songbird in the New World warbler family, found in North and Central America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The olive-capped warbler (Setophaga pityophila) is a species of New World warbler that is native to the western and eastern ends of Cuba as well as Grand Bahama and the Abaco Islands in the Bahamas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: pictus | synonyms = Setophaga pictus }} The painted redstart or painted whitestart (Myioborus pictus) is a species of New World warbler found in mountainous areas across inland Central America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The slate-throated whitestart (Myioborus miniatus), also known as the slate-throated redstart, is a small insectivorous bird in the New World warbler family Parulidae that is native to Central and South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: peregrina }} The Tennessee warbler (Leiothlypis peregrina) is a New World warbler that breeds in eastern North America and winters in southern Central America, the Caribbean, and northern South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: dominica }} The yellow-throated warbler (Setophaga dominica) is a small migratory songbird species in the New World warbler family (Parulidae) found in temperate North America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The worm-eating warbler (Helmitheros vermivorum) is a small New World warbler that breeds in the Eastern United States and migrates to southern Mexico, the Caribbean, and Central America for the winter
